summary: The `useEffect` hooks in `CartView.jsx` clean up correctly, but I found three new issues: the loyalty panel can crash on a nullish `customerEmail`, table-code verification cannot retry the same code after a failed attempt, and the reward-email flow leaves a timeout running after unmount.

findings.title: Loyalty section assumes `customerEmail` is always a string
findings.description: The loyalty render path calls `customerEmail.trim()` directly in multiple branches. Earlier in the component the same prop is treated as optional (`customerEmail && String(customerEmail).trim()`), so if the parent initializes it as `null`/`undefined`, opening this section throws before the cart can render.
findings.suggested_fix: Normalize the prop once, e.g. `const safeCustomerEmail = typeof customerEmail === "string" ? customerEmail : "";`, and use that normalized value for `value`, `.trim()`, and related conditionals.

findings.title: Auto-verify permanently suppresses retries for the same table code
findings.description: `lastSentVerifyCodeRef` is used as a permanent dedupe key, but it is never cleared on verification failure or after cooldown reset. Because this drawer has no manual verify action, any retryable failure for a correct code leaves the user unable to resubmit that same code until the component is remounted.
findings.suggested_fix: Only dedupe while a request is in flight, or clear `lastSentVerifyCodeRef.current` when verification completes with an error, when the input becomes incomplete, or when the cooldown expires.

findings.title: Reward-email success timer is not cleaned up on unmount
findings.description: The reward-email save handler starts a `setTimeout` that later calls `setRewardEmailSubmitting(false)` and `setShowRewardEmailForm(false)`, but the timer id is never stored or cleared. If the drawer closes before the delay completes, the callback still fires against an unmounted component.
findings.suggested_fix: Store the timeout id in a ref and clear it from a cleanup effect, or remove the delayed state update entirely and drive the success state without a raw timer.

missing_tests: Render the loyalty-expanded cart with `customerEmail` undefined/null and verify it does not crash.
Simulate a retryable `verifyTableCode` failure and confirm the same full table code can be submitted again without remounting the drawer.
Save the reward email, unmount the cart before the 1 second delay elapses, and assert no post-unmount state update occurs.
notes: Bundle-only review; no additional files were needed.

All five `useEffect` hooks in `CartView.jsx` were inspected: the interval effect and the debounced timeout effect both return cleanup functions, and there are no `addEventListener` subscriptions in this file.
The bundle reported `No BUGS.md found`, so none of the findings were filtered out as already known.
